check_content_excerpt:
Verify Ubuntu 24.04 LTS is not configured to reboot the system when Ctrl-Alt-Delete is pressed when using a graphical user interface with the following command:

$ gsettings get org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.media-keys logout
@as []

If the "logout" key is bound to an action, is commented out, or is missing, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure the system to disable the Ctrl-Alt-Delete sequence when using a graphical user interface.

gsettings set org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.media-keys logout []

Update the dconf settings:

$ sudo dconf update
title: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must disable the x86 Ctrl-Alt-Delete key sequence if a graphical user interface is installed.
